For Arbitration
Off Calendar May-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NIESAR & VESTAL LLP, GERALD NIESAR's PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION AND STAYING ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION

Defendants Niesar & Vestal LLP and Gerald Niesar's petition to compel arbitration is GRANTED; the action is stayed pending arbitration.

Whether an arbitration agreement exists is a question to be decided by the court. (Baker v. Italian Maple Holdings, LLC (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 1152, 1158.)

Niesar proves that plaintiff Mary Ellen Petroni reviewed and signed the fee agreement, confirmed the retention in a subsequent letter and made an initial payment under the hourly rates stated in the agreement. (Niesar Dec. 3-5, 7 Exs. A-B; Sbona Dec. 9-11; Earsom Dec. 2.) Petroni does not deny signing the agreement or possessing it. (Petroni Dec. 2-3.)

The agreement does not unmistakably delegate issues of arbitrability or voidability to the arbitrator. (Ajamian v. CantorCO2e, L.P. (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 771, 791.) Thus whether the agreement is voidable under Business and Professions Code 6148 is for the court to decide. Niesar's failure to get a copy of the executed agreement does not frustrate 6148's purpose, which is to ensure that the client understands and consents to the fee agreement. (National Parks & Conservation Assn. v. County of Riverside (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 1505, 1522.)
The agreement is not unenforceable as a result of Petroni not delivering an executed copy to Niesar. The agreement's language does not prescribe delivery of the contract as a condition precedent for its effect. Rather, it says attorneys "may not be able to begin work" until receiving the executed copy.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

For Arbitration
